zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Harcourt於大約1200年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓時，他的父親William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle26歲，母親Alice Noell36歲。他和Orabella Quincy至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1258年4月2日在英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯享年59歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾布拉可利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Harcourt was born about 1200, in Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle, was 26 and his mother, Alice Noell, was 36. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Orabella Quincy. He died on 2 April 1258, in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Brackley, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de Harcourt est né environ 1200, à Wormleighton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alice Noell, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Orabella Quincy. Il est décédé le 2 avril 1258, à Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Brackley, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de Harcourt um 1200 in Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle 26 und seine Mutter Alice Noell 36. Er hatte mit Orabella Quincy mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 2. April 1258 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England und wurde in Brackley, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Harcourtが約1200年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウオルムレイトンで生まれた時，父William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castleは26歳，母Alice Noellは36歳であった。彼はOrabella Quincyとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1258年4月2日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルスで59歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブラックリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Harcourt(이)가 대략 1200년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워레이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Noell(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Orabella Quincy(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1258년 4월 2일 영국 레이스스터실르 마크트 보스워스에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 브라클리에 묻혔다.

Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Orabella Quincy. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Harcourt родился около 1200 г. в местности вормлиитон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Harcourt Governor of Tamworth Castle было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Noell, 36. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Orabella Quincy. Он умер 2 апреля 1258 г. в местности маркет босворт, лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности браккли, нортамптоншир, Англия.
